title: Slow Clients, Resource Limits, Backpressure, Admission, and Cleanup · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective connection resource budget and overload policy; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in connection resource budget and overload policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound start-line, header, content, trailer, idle, and total-phase time using both progress and absolute budgets.
Bound per-connection buffers, retained slices, field maps, content queues, trailer data, and pooled-object capacity before allocation.
Propagate slow peer writes through bounded queues, cancellation, deadlines, partial output evidence, and producer shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Slow Header Body Defense
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug protocol team handles case 5726 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine absolute phase time, memory, and output-queue budgets for slow-peer defense; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound start-line, header, content, trailer, idle, and total-phase time using both progress and absolute budgets.

Inspect the Slow Header Body Defense trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Slow Header Body Defense.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Slow Header Body Defense, keep Slow Header Body Defense connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Resource Cleanup Proof
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-resource-cleanup-proof-11vse2" that uses case 8922 to combine absolute phase time, memory, and output-queue budgets for slow-peer defense. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Resource Cleanup Proof.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Resource Cleanup Proof into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Resource Cleanup Proof, keep Resource Cleanup Proof connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove connection, goroutine, timer, buffer, queue, tunnel, temporary data, and metric-cardinality cleanup for every terminal state.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Resource Cleanup Proof, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Resource Cleanup Proof precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug Resource Cleanup Proof, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug protocol team handles case 5734 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine absolute phase time, memory, and output-queue budgets for slow-peer defense; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove connection, goroutine, timer, buffer, queue, tunnel, temporary data, and metric-cardinality cleanup for every terminal state.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Resource Cleanup Proof, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Defers guarantee cleanup finishes within budget after every timeout and forced shutdown.
